_comment: Authored from Document 8 Section 5 (Weekly Signal Monitoring). Document 8 §5.1 states the cycle 'covers eight checks'; §5.2 enumerates exactly Check 1 through Check 8; §5.3's escalation reference table lists exactly 8 rows. There is no corpus concept of a session-open/session-close bookend step. The prior 10-step version (steps 1 and 10 as structural bookends) was corrected to 8 steps to match the corpus exactly.
_phase_convention: phase is a required numeric field on WorkflowStepDefinition (no '?'), unused by any runtime logic or UI. Monitoring is NOT a lifecycle-phased workflow — unlike Commissioning's Generate/Review/Approve/Publish-Sync/Closure structure, the corpus describes Monitoring's 8 checks as parallel, not phased (Document 8 §5.1: 'executed weekly... in parallel'). All 8 steps use phase: 0 as a documented not-applicable placeholder satisfying the required field — this is a DIFFERENT MEANING than Commissioning's phase: 0, which marks 'pre-Phase-1 step in a workflow that DOES have phases 1-4.' Do not transfer Commissioning's reading onto Monitoring: Monitoring's 0 means 'this workflow has no phase structure,' not 'before phase 1.'

steps.input_required: Content Ops Lead queries the bg_stage distribution report (AEP-sourced operational dashboard) for account count per bg_stage per active solution category, as a percentage of total active TAL accounts in that category. Time-boxed to 5 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: no solution category is at or above a commission threshold without an active commissioning sprint already in progress for the corresponding brief type. Escalation triggers on either of two distinct thresholds, evaluated independently per solution category.
steps.why_this_matters: This check is the trigger mechanism for new commissioning sprints — it is the operational link between buying-group movement (accounts advancing through bg_stage) and content supply. Missing this check's escalation means accounts could advance into qualified or prioritized stages with no converge content sprint underway to support them, leaving Champions and Economic Buyers without the consensus_brief or executive_brief content the buying group dynamics at that stage require.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires if any solution category has 20% or more of active TAL accounts at engaged or prioritized bg_stage with no active consensus_brief commissioning sprint in progress. Next action: route to Section 4.2 (Converge Commission Triggers) and initiate a consensus_brief sprint; record the solution category, triggering metric, and sprint initiation date in the weekly review log.

Fires if any solution category has 10% or more of active TAL accounts at qualified bg_stage with no active executive_brief commissioning sprint in progress. Next action: route to Section 4.2 and initiate an executive_brief sprint; record the solution category, triggering metric, and sprint initiation date in the weekly review log.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires if any solution category has 20% or more of active TAL accounts at engaged or prioritized bg_stage with no active consensus_brief commissioning sprint in progress. Next action: route to Section 4.2 (Converge Commission Triggers) and initiate a consensus_brief sprint; record the solution category, triggering metric, and sprint initiation date in the weekly review log.

Fires if any solution category has 10% or more of active TAL accounts at qualified bg_stage with no active executive_brief commissioning sprint in progress. Next action: route to Section 4.2 and initiate an executive_brief sprint; record the solution category, triggering metric, and sprint initiation date in the weekly review log.

steps.deferral_consequence: Cannot be deferred. If this check is skipped, a solution category may cross a commission threshold with no sprint initiated, and the gap will not surface until a later check or a sales activation failure makes it visible — by which point the buying group has already progressed without supporting content.
steps._note: TWO-THRESHOLD TOPOLOGY: the corpus's single 'Escalation condition' paragraph contains two independently-evaluated sub-conditions tied to two different content types (consensus_brief at engaged/prioritized ≥20%; executive_brief at qualified ≥10%) with two different remedies (which sprint type to initiate) — split into H-01/H-02 per the divergent-remedy/distinguishable-failure principle. Reuses the dormant-gate convention established in Commissioning since the same evaluator defect applies identically. ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION: step_id 1 collides with Onboarding step_id 1 (tal_domain, validation_type: None, hold codes ['H-01'], flag codes []) — Commissioning step_id 1 had no gates authored, so there is no prior Monitoring/Commissioning-side reuse available here; this is the first place tal_domain's collision is actually exercised against authored content. evaluateH01's only special cases are step_id===10/17; step_id 1 falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION for both H-01 and H-02 here, since neither corpus condition (percentage threshold breaches) has any relationship to null-ness. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived, not assumed): H-02 would additionally route through evaluateH02's boolean-type fallback branch (tal_domain has no validation_type, so no mapping_table/enum branch applies) — fails-closed-spurious, near-always-true for the non-boolean percentage-threshold payload this code is meant to carry. category: A because both thresholds, when breached, name a specific required remediation action (initiate a sprint) with no documented-exception path, matching the Commissioning precedent for corpus-described required action → category A. No FLAG described in the corpus for this check.

steps.input_required: Analytics Lead queries the solution_category_coverage_status AEP attribute (§CA Section 2.2.1) for all five solution categories via the operational dashboard, and cross-checks against Sanity tuple-level coverage_status data per (solution_category, role, buying_stage) tuple via direct Sanity query. Time-boxed to 10 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: the AEP attribute value and the Sanity tuple-level rollup agree for all five solution categories. Any discrepancy for any category is an escalation condition.
steps.why_this_matters: This check is the cross-system integrity verification between the content graph (Sanity) and the activation layer (AEP/Target) — it confirms the Commissioning sync pipeline (coverage status recomputation, Document 8 §3.7) actually propagated correctly. Without this check, a stale AEP attribute could leave Adobe Target serving against an outdated coverage assumption while Sanity already reflects updated coverage.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires if the solution_category_coverage_status AEP attribute shows a different status than the Sanity tuple-level rollup for any solution category. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer — the coverage tracking pipeline may have missed a Sanity webhook event (Document 8 §3.7, D8-Flag-02). Document the discrepancy, affected solution category, and the timestamp of the last confirmed sync in the escalation record.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires if the solution_category_coverage_status AEP attribute shows a different status than the Sanity tuple-level rollup for any solution category. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer — the coverage tracking pipeline may have missed a Sanity webhook event (Document 8 §3.7, D8-Flag-02). Document the discrepancy, affected solution category, and the timestamp of the last confirmed sync in the escalation record.

steps.deferral_consequence: Cannot be deferred. An undetected AEP/Sanity coverage discrepancy means Adobe Target may serve against stale coverage data indefinitely until the next weekly check happens to catch it.
steps._note: SINGLE-CONDITION TOPOLOGY: one comparison, one escalation path — one HOLD code. ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION (reused from Commissioning step_id 2's analysis): step_id 2 collides with Onboarding step_id 2 (tal_member, validation_type: None, hold codes ['H-01','H-02'], flag codes ['F-01']). evaluateH01's only special cases are step_id===10/17; step_id 2 falls through to isNullish(value). MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived, verified independently): MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION — null-ness has no relationship to an AEP/Sanity coverage_status mismatch. This Monitoring step authors only H-01 (Commissioning step 2 also only authored H-01, for an unrelated reason — Gate-Platform-Engineer-confirmation there vs. AEP/Sanity-mismatch here), so the finding is independently confirmed for this step's actual condition, not inherited by code-reuse alone. category: A because Document 8 names a specific, required escalation action (to Platform Engineer) with no documented-exception path. No FLAG described in the corpus for this check.

steps.input_required: Analytics Lead reviews the 7-day rolling pending_solution_fallback event count per solution category via the operational monitoring dashboard (Snowflake-sourced). Compares each category's count to its calibrated escalation threshold. Starting value (verified against kalder_data_model.py §4 SCORING_RULES): 50 events per 7-day window, applies until 30-day post-v1-launch calibration completes. Calibration procedure (Document 4 §7.4.2): observe event volume for 30 days without alerting, compute the 7-day rolling average, set the per-category threshold at 2x that baseline with a minimum floor of 20 events per 7 days (below the floor, alerts are informational only — volume too low to prioritize a sprint), apply the 20-event floor to categories with zero observed events (expected for employee_experience, risk_compliance, ai_platform at v1 launch), and review thresholds at 60 days and quarterly thereafter. Time-boxed to 10 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: all solution categories are below their calibrated escalation threshold. For any category that exceeded its threshold during the week, confirm the automatic alert to slack_data_team_channel already fired per Document 4 §7.4.3 — the alert payload must include solution_category, current_coverage_status, fallback_event_count_7d, and gap_summary.
steps.why_this_matters: This check verifies both that the content gap escalation pipeline (Document 4 §7.4) is itself functioning and that any real coverage gap it surfaces actually gets routed to remediation. A silent failure here is doubly dangerous: if the automatic alert mechanism breaks, this weekly check is the only remaining backstop that would ever catch a solution category falling behind on coverage.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires if any solution category exceeds its calibrated threshold AND no automatic alert was received in slack_data_team_channel for that category — the escalation mechanism itself has failed. Next action: escalate immediately to Platform Engineer; the automatic alert pipeline must be restored before the next weekly review. Log the affected category and threshold breach date.

Fires if any solution category exceeds its calibrated threshold AND the automatic alert did fire — a real coverage gap requiring remediation. Next action: route to Section 7 for coverage gap sprint prioritization, using the gap_summary field from the alert payload (Document 4 §7.4.3) to identify which (role, buying_stage) combinations and module types to produce.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires if any solution category exceeds its calibrated threshold AND no automatic alert was received in slack_data_team_channel for that category — the escalation mechanism itself has failed. Next action: escalate immediately to Platform Engineer; the automatic alert pipeline must be restored before the next weekly review. Log the affected category and threshold breach date.

Fires if any solution category exceeds its calibrated threshold AND the automatic alert did fire — a real coverage gap requiring remediation. Next action: route to Section 7 for coverage gap sprint prioritization, using the gap_summary field from the alert payload (Document 4 §7.4.3) to identify which (role, buying_stage) combinations and module types to produce.

steps.deferral_consequence: Cannot be deferred. Skipping this check risks an undetected alert-pipeline failure persisting silently — the corpus names this the most severe of the two escalation conditions precisely because no other mechanism would catch it.
steps._note: VERIFIED NUMERIC VALUE: the 50-events/7-days starting threshold, the 30-day calibration window, the 2x rolling-average formula, and the 20-event floor were each checked directly against Document 4 §7.4.2 AND kalder_data_model.py's §4 SCORING_RULES.pending_solution_fallback.escalation_threshold (fallback_event_count: 50, window_days: 7) — both sources agree exactly. Treated with the same verbatim-citation care as Step 10's GROQ error strings, since this is an operator-acted-upon number, not descriptive prose. TWO-CONDITION TOPOLOGY (same shape as Step 1, different content): both (a) alert-pipeline-failure and (b) real-gap-with-alert-fired carry required Escalation action language, not a documented-exception path — two HOLDs, no FLAG. ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION (reused from Commissioning step_id 3's analysis): step_id 3 collides with Onboarding step_id 3 (tal_account_type_source, validation_type: mapping_table, hold codes ['H-01','H-02'], flag codes ['F-06']). CHECKED, NOT ASSUMED IRRELEVANT: evaluateF06 has a real step_id===3 special case (checks submission.practitionerOverrodeAdvisorSuggestion === true, tied to Onboarding's mapping-table-override flag) — confirmed this does not affect this step's authored content because no FLAG/F-06 is authored here. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived): evaluateH01 falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION, unrelated to threshold/alert-status. evaluateH02 with validation_type: mapping_table routes to the allowed_values-membership branch; this Monitoring step defines no allowed_values, so membership against an empty array is always-true-for-non-null — FAILS-CLOSED-SPURIOUS, same shape as Commissioning step 3's H-02 finding, independently re-confirmed. category: A because both escalation conditions name required, non-optional remediation actions. No FLAG described in the corpus for this check.

steps.corpus_authority: Document 8 §5.2 Check 4; §CA Section 2.2.2 stitching_pending field definition (24-hour SLA)
steps.input_required: Marketing Ops Engineer queries AEP contact profiles where stitching_pending = True (§CA Section 2.2.2) via AEP segment query or operational dashboard, filtering for records where the stitching initiation timestamp exceeds 24 hours from the time of query. Time-boxed to 5 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: no contact has stitching_pending = True persisting beyond 24 hours, and the automatic 24-hour SLA alert (per §CA Section 2.2.2) was received for any contact that did breach. Escalation condition: any contact with stitching_pending = True persisting beyond 24 hours, regardless of whether the automatic alert fired — alert firing does not resolve the breach if stitching has not completed.
steps.why_this_matters: stitching_pending blocks identity resolution between anonymous and known visitor records — a contact stuck in this state cannot be reliably matched to its account or role classification, which means role-confidence scoring and sales activation gating (Check 8) cannot operate correctly for that contact until stitching resolves. This check is the only place a stalled stitching job gets caught within a week of occurring.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires if any contact has stitching_pending = True persisting beyond 24 hours, regardless of whether the automatic SLA alert fired. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer with the affected contact_id and stitching initiation timestamp; the Platform Engineer investigates the Kafka pipeline or AEP identity stitching job, and resolution must be confirmed before the next weekly review. If the automatic alert did not fire, include that as a secondary pipeline failure in the same escalation record — this is detail within one escalation, not a second independently-firing condition.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires if any contact has stitching_pending = True persisting beyond 24 hours, regardless of whether the automatic SLA alert fired. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer with the affected contact_id and stitching initiation timestamp; the Platform Engineer investigates the Kafka pipeline or AEP identity stitching job, and resolution must be confirmed before the next weekly review. If the automatic alert did not fire, include that as a secondary pipeline failure in the same escalation record — this is detail within one escalation, not a second independently-firing condition.

steps.deferral_consequence: Cannot be deferred. An unresolved stitching_pending breach blocks identity resolution for the affected contact indefinitely until caught — there is no other monitoring mechanism named in the corpus that independently surfaces this.
steps._note: VERIFIED VALUES: the 24-hour SLA is stated identically in two independent corpus locations (§CA Section 2.2.2 field definition: 'a 24-hour SLA applies'; §5.2 Check 4: 'exceeds 24 hours' / 'persisting beyond 24 hours', stated three times) — both agree exactly, no correction needed. The 5-minute time-box is taken directly from the Check 4 header. SINGLE-CONDITION TOPOLOGY with an embedded secondary-detail clause: unlike Step 3's two-independently-firing-conditions split, Check 4 describes one breach condition (persisting beyond 24h) with a notable detail (alert-fired-or-not) to be logged within the same escalation record, not a second condition with its own distinct remedy — one HOLD, no FLAG. Contrast named explicitly because the two shapes look superficially similar (both checks have an 'alert fired or not' dimension) but resolve differently: Step 3's two outcomes have genuinely different remedies (restore-pipeline vs. prioritize-coverage-sprint); Step 4's alert-status is just supporting detail for one remedy (escalate to Platform Engineer either way). ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION (reused from Commissioning step_id 4's analysis): step_id 4 collides with Onboarding step_id 4 (tal_program_status, validation_type: mapping_table, hold codes ['H-01','H-02'], flag codes ['F-01','F-01'] — VERIFIED not a transcription artifact: Onboarding step 4's flag_triggers array genuinely contains two separate F-01 entries with distinct condition/consequence text, one for post_sale and one for out_of_program). CHECKED, NOT ASSUMED IRRELEVANT: evaluateF01 has a real step_id===4 special case (value === 'post_sale' || value === 'out_of_program') — confirmed this does not affect this step's authored content because no FLAG/F-01 is authored here; Check 4 has no documented-exception path warranting a FLAG. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived): evaluateH01 falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION, unrelated to a 24-hour SLA breach. category: A because the corpus names a required, non-optional escalation action with no documented-exception path.

steps.corpus_authority: Document 8 §5.2 Check 5; §CA Section 2.2.1 tal_last_refreshed_at field definition (72-hour PER-ACCOUNT staleness threshold — defined, distinct from the WEEKLY ESCALATION threshold below). Document 8 §12 Incident Response Procedures (read in full, subsections 12.1–12.6) — does NOT define the weekly stale-account-count escalation threshold that §5.2 Check 5 forward-references; this second threshold is a corpus gap.
steps.input_required: Marketing Ops Engineer queries AEP account profiles where tal_last_refreshed_at (§CA Section 2.2.1) exceeds 72 hours from the time of query, via operational dashboard, and compares the stale account count to the prior week's count. Time-boxed to 5 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: stale account count is zero or within the historical trend baseline established in the first four weeks of production operation, and week-over-week count is stable. Three possible outcomes: (a) single-week spike exceeding a weekly-escalation threshold the corpus names but does not define (CORPUS GAP — see below); (b) week-over-week rise for three consecutive weekly reviews, independent of (a); (c) neither — log the count and monitor next week.
steps.why_this_matters: tal_last_refreshed_at staleness means an account's TAL data is no longer current — Document 8 explicitly states this field is 'not read by Target... staleness monitoring input only,' so a stale account doesn't break live personalization directly, but it signals the Kafka pipeline feeding TAL data may be degrading, which left unchecked could eventually affect every downstream attribute (tal_member, tal_program_status, etc.) sourced from the same sync.

hold_triggers.condition: TWO DISTINCT THRESHOLDS — do not conflate. The 72-hour PER-ACCOUNT threshold (what makes one account 'stale') IS defined, in §CA Section 2.2.1, and is already applied in input_required's query. This HOLD concerns a SEPARATE, second threshold: the WEEKLY STALE-ACCOUNT-COUNT escalation threshold — Document 8 §5.2 Check 5 names this as 'the threshold defined in Section 12 (Incident Response)', but Section 12's six subsections (12.2-12.6: Signal Collection Failure, Classification Accuracy Drop, Content Node Error, Sync Pipeline Failure, Outreach Alert Payload) contain no TAL-staleness or Kafka-identity-stitching incident scenario, and no numeric count value is defined anywhere in Document 8. This is a forward reference to a definition that does not exist in the corpus as provided — it blocks only this HOLD, not H-02 or F-01 below. Authored condition (pending threshold definition): fires if the single-week stale ACCOUNT COUNT exceeds [WEEKLY ESCALATION THRESHOLD UNDEFINED — §12 gap]. Next action when it does fire: initiate Section 12 incident response immediately, and escalate to Platform Engineer for Kafka pipeline investigation in parallel — this remedy IS specified even though the triggering count threshold is not. Action for the corpus owner: author the missing weekly-count threshold into §12, or correct the §5.2 cross-reference to point to wherever it is actually defined.

FULLY OPERATIONALIZABLE (no gap): fires if the stale account count rises week-over-week for three consecutive weekly reviews, regardless of whether H-01's (undefined) weekly-count threshold has been reached. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer for Kafka pipeline investigation; log the three-week trend with week-over-week counts in the escalation record. Section 12 incident response is not triggered by this condition alone — only by H-01's threshold breach.

flag_triggers.condition: FULLY OPERATIONALIZABLE (no gap): fires when neither H-01 nor H-02's condition is met but a single-week spike occurred below H-01's (undefined) weekly-count threshold with no three-week trend. This is the corpus's named normal-monitoring outcome, not a failure state.
flag_triggers.consequence: Log the count and monitor next week. No escalation action.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: TWO DISTINCT THRESHOLDS — do not conflate. The 72-hour PER-ACCOUNT threshold (what makes one account 'stale') IS defined, in §CA Section 2.2.1, and is already applied in input_required's query. This HOLD concerns a SEPARATE, second threshold: the WEEKLY STALE-ACCOUNT-COUNT escalation threshold — Document 8 §5.2 Check 5 names this as 'the threshold defined in Section 12 (Incident Response)', but Section 12's six subsections (12.2-12.6: Signal Collection Failure, Classification Accuracy Drop, Content Node Error, Sync Pipeline Failure, Outreach Alert Payload) contain no TAL-staleness or Kafka-identity-stitching incident scenario, and no numeric count value is defined anywhere in Document 8. This is a forward reference to a definition that does not exist in the corpus as provided — it blocks only this HOLD, not H-02 or F-01 below. Authored condition (pending threshold definition): fires if the single-week stale ACCOUNT COUNT exceeds [WEEKLY ESCALATION THRESHOLD UNDEFINED — §12 gap]. Next action when it does fire: initiate Section 12 incident response immediately, and escalate to Platform Engineer for Kafka pipeline investigation in parallel — this remedy IS specified even though the triggering count threshold is not. Action for the corpus owner: author the missing weekly-count threshold into §12, or correct the §5.2 cross-reference to point to wherever it is actually defined.

FULLY OPERATIONALIZABLE (no gap): fires if the stale account count rises week-over-week for three consecutive weekly reviews, regardless of whether H-01's (undefined) weekly-count threshold has been reached. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer for Kafka pipeline investigation; log the three-week trend with week-over-week counts in the escalation record. Section 12 incident response is not triggered by this condition alone — only by H-01's threshold breach.

flag_triggers_pending.condition: FULLY OPERATIONALIZABLE (no gap): fires when neither H-01 nor H-02's condition is met but a single-week spike occurred below H-01's (undefined) weekly-count threshold with no three-week trend. This is the corpus's named normal-monitoring outcome, not a failure state.
flag_triggers_pending.consequence: Log the count and monitor next week. No escalation action.

steps._note: CORPUS GAP FOUND, NAMED, AND SCOPED PRECISELY — two thresholds exist in this check, only one is missing: (1) the 72-HOUR PER-ACCOUNT staleness threshold (§CA Section 2.2.1) — DEFINED, used directly in input_required's query, fully operationalizable; (2) the WEEKLY STALE-ACCOUNT-COUNT ESCALATION threshold that §5.2 Check 5 forward-references to 'Section 12' — UNDEFINED, Section 12 (read in full) contains no TAL-staleness incident scenario and no numeric count value. These must not be conflated: finding the 72-hour value does not resolve the gap, because the gap is in a different, second threshold entirely. The gap blocks ONLY H-01 (the spike gate) — H-02 (trend) and F-01 (normal-monitoring outcome) are fully operationalizable as authored, since neither depends on the missing number. THREE-OUTCOME TOPOLOGY (new shape, not seen in Steps 1-4): the corpus names three mutually exclusive outcomes explicitly — (a) spike-over-undefined-threshold [escalate hard], (b) three-week trend [escalate softer, independent of (a)], (c) neither [log and monitor, the corpus's own stated non-escalation outcome] — authored as H-01/H-02/F-01 respectively. F-01 is the FIRST FLAG in Monitoring authored for a genuinely normal, expected, non-failure outcome the corpus names explicitly, distinct from Commissioning Step 12's F-01 (a documented-exception path for an otherwise-incomplete sprint). ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION (reused from Commissioning step_id 5's analysis): step_id 5 collides with Onboarding step_id 5 (tal_region, validation_type: None, hold codes ['H-01'], flag codes ['F-04']). CHECKED, NOT ASSUMED IRRELEVANT: evaluateF04 has a real step_id===5 special case (GDPR-jurisdiction heuristic via practitionerOverrodeAdvisorSuggestion, tied to Onboarding's tal_region) — confirmed irrelevant to this step's content since this step's FLAG (F-01) uses a different code entirely; F-04 would not even be invoked here. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived): evaluateH01 falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION, unrelated to a stale-count threshold breach. evaluateF01's step_id checks are ===2/4/18 only; step_id 5 matches none, falls through to the function's bare final return false — FAILS-OPEN, the first FLAG fails-open finding in Monitoring, same shape as Commissioning Step 12's F-01 finding. category: A because H-01/H-02 name required escalation actions despite the threshold gap in H-01.

steps.corpus_authority: Document 8 §5.2 Check 6; §CA Section 2.4 (multi_match_unresolved excluded as monitoring-only, not read by any activation channel)
steps.input_required: Marketing Ops Engineer queries AEP anonymous identifier records where multi_match_unresolved = True via operational dashboard, noting count, record age distribution, and whether any affected records are associated with accounts at bg_stage: qualified. Time-boxed to 5 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: count is stable week-over-week, no records older than 14 days, and no records associated with accounts at bg_stage: qualified. Two independently-evaluated escalation conditions, each with a distinct remedy.
steps.why_this_matters: multi_match_unresolved is explicitly excluded from §CA as a monitoring-only attribute not read by any activation channel — it has no direct runtime effect on personalization. Its risk is entirely indirect: an unresolved match at a qualified-stage account means a Champion's behavioral signals may be triggering convergence point alerts while the Economic Buyer's contact record can't be stitched to receive corresponding treatment, undermining the buying-group-level coherence this program depends on.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires if any multi_match_unresolved record is associated with an account at bg_stage: qualified, regardless of record age. Document 8 §5.2 names this 'the highest-priority condition in Check 6.' Next action: escalate immediately to Platform Engineer. Late-stage accounts with unresolved contact stitching have active convergence point alert exposure — a Champion at a qualified-stage account may be triggering alerts while the economic buyer's contact record cannot be stitched.

Fires if the multi_match_unresolved count rises week-over-week for three consecutive weekly reviews, when condition (a)/H-01 has NOT also fired. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer for Salesforce duplicate contact investigation — rising counts typically indicate duplicate contact records in the CRM requiring CRM-side cleanup before AEP resolution criteria can match uniquely.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires if any multi_match_unresolved record is associated with an account at bg_stage: qualified, regardless of record age. Document 8 §5.2 names this 'the highest-priority condition in Check 6.' Next action: escalate immediately to Platform Engineer. Late-stage accounts with unresolved contact stitching have active convergence point alert exposure — a Champion at a qualified-stage account may be triggering alerts while the economic buyer's contact record cannot be stitched.

Fires if the multi_match_unresolved count rises week-over-week for three consecutive weekly reviews, when condition (a)/H-01 has NOT also fired. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer for Salesforce duplicate contact investigation — rising counts typically indicate duplicate contact records in the CRM requiring CRM-side cleanup before AEP resolution criteria can match uniquely.

steps.deferral_consequence: Cannot be deferred. Condition (a) in particular is named the highest-priority condition in this check precisely because it represents live alert exposure at a late buying stage — deferring this check risks a Champion-side alert firing with no corresponding Economic Buyer treatment for an arbitrary length of time.
steps._note: TWO-CONDITION TOPOLOGY (same shape as Step 3's a/b split — both conditions independently-firing with distinct remedies), but with an explicit PRIORITY ORDERING the corpus states directly ('the highest-priority condition in Check 6' for (a)) rather than leaving relative severity implicit — both H-01 and H-02 can in principle co-occur, but H-02's condition text notes it applies 'when (a) has NOT also fired' per the corpus's 'For (b) without (a)' framing, mirroring Step 5's H-02 'without (a)' structure. NO CORPUS GAP THIS TIME: both numeric thresholds (14-day record age ceiling in the normal-result baseline, 3-week trend window in H-02) are explicit and defined — contrast directly with Step 5's undefined weekly-count threshold. ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION: step_id 6 collides with Onboarding step_id 6 (tal_marquee, validation_type: None, hold codes ['H-02'], flag codes ['F-02']). CHECKED: no step_id===6 special case exists anywhere in flag-hold-evaluator.ts for ANY evaluator function — confirmed by direct grep, not assumed; this is a pure generic-fallthrough collision for both codes. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived, corrected mid-draft to dispatch on THIS step's code rather than Onboarding step 6's code inventory): this step's H-01 dispatches to evaluateH01 (Onboarding step 6 has no H-01 of its own, but dispatch is by CODE not by which codes the colliding step happens to use) — falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION, unrelated to qualified-stage record association. This step's H-02 dispatches to evaluateH02; Onboarding step 6 (tal_marquee) has validation_type: None, so evaluateH02 routes to the boolean-type fallback branch — FAILS-CLOSED-SPURIOUS, near-always-true for the non-boolean trend-count payload H-02 is meant to carry. category: A because both conditions name required, non-optional escalation actions with no documented-exception path. No FLAG described in the corpus for this check.

steps.input_required: Analytics Lead compares the current week's role_confidence_score (§CA Section 2.2.2) and confidence_tier distribution for classified contacts (via Snowflake or operational dashboard) against the prior week's proportion of contacts at LOW or UNKNOWN confidence tier, per role. Time-boxed to 10 minutes per the corpus. The weekly LOW/UNKNOWN distribution snapshot must be preserved regardless of outcome — it feeds the T2-06 monthly Role Classification Accuracy computation (Document 7) and must not be discarded after review.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: LOW/UNKNOWN proportion is stable week-over-week within the established monitoring threshold. Two-tier escalation: Tier 1 (log-and-monitor) fires on a single-week shift meeting either the 5-percentage-point-absolute OR the 20%-relative criterion alone, for any role. Tier 2 (escalate) fires on either two-consecutive-week persistence of a Tier-1-level shift, or a single-week shift exceeding 10 percentage points absolute.
steps.why_this_matters: A rising LOW/UNKNOWN proportion means role classification is degrading — visitors who should be resolving to a confident role classification are not. This directly threatens the personalization program's core mechanism (role-based content selection) and, via the T2-06 monthly bridge, is a required input to long-term classification accuracy tracking — discarding a weekly snapshot doesn't just lose this week's signal, it creates a permanent gap in the monthly and quarterly trend data.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires (Tier 2 — escalate) if a Tier-1-level LOW/UNKNOWN shift (5pp absolute or 20% relative) persists for two consecutive weekly reviews, OR if a single-week shift exceeds 10 percentage points absolute for any role. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer. Signal collection failure or signal weight drift investigation required. Provide the prior two weeks' distribution snapshots with the escalation record.

flag_triggers.condition: Fires (Tier 1 — log and monitor) if, for any role, the LOW/UNKNOWN proportion increases by more than 5 percentage points absolute OR more than 20% relative from the prior week's baseline, as a single-week shift not yet meeting Tier 2's persistence or magnitude bar. This is the corpus's own first-tier, non-escalating outcome.
flag_triggers.consequence: Log the shift and monitor next week. No escalation action at this tier.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires (Tier 2 — escalate) if a Tier-1-level LOW/UNKNOWN shift (5pp absolute or 20% relative) persists for two consecutive weekly reviews, OR if a single-week shift exceeds 10 percentage points absolute for any role. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer. Signal collection failure or signal weight drift investigation required. Provide the prior two weeks' distribution snapshots with the escalation record.

flag_triggers_pending.condition: Fires (Tier 1 — log and monitor) if, for any role, the LOW/UNKNOWN proportion increases by more than 5 percentage points absolute OR more than 20% relative from the prior week's baseline, as a single-week shift not yet meeting Tier 2's persistence or magnitude bar. This is the corpus's own first-tier, non-escalating outcome.
flag_triggers_pending.consequence: Log the shift and monitor next week. No escalation action at this tier.

steps._note: TWO-TIER SEVERITY TOPOLOGY (new shape, distinct from Steps 1/3/6's two-INDEPENDENT-condition splits): this is not two parallel conditions with different remedies, it is ONE metric (LOW/UNKNOWN proportion shift) with two SEVERITY THRESHOLDS on the same underlying signal — Tier 1 (single-week, smaller magnitude) logs; Tier 2 (two-week persistence OR larger single-week magnitude) escalates. Authored as F-01 (Tier 1) + H-01 (Tier 2), not two HOLDs, because Tier 1 is explicitly the corpus's own non-escalating, named outcome ('log and monitor next week'), matching Step 5's F-01 FLAG-for-normal-outcome pattern, not a failure state. WORDING PARSED CAREFULLY: the corpus's 'more than 5pp absolute AND more than 20% relative... whichever is smaller triggers logging' initially reads as a conjunctive AND requirement, but the immediately following sentence ('A single-week shift meeting EITHER criterion alone: log and monitor') resolves this as a disjunctive OR — either threshold alone is sufficient for Tier 1; the 'whichever is smaller' clause describes which of the two bars is the operative (more sensitive) one, not an additional joint requirement. Authored per this corrected reading, not the surface-level AND misreading. MONTHLY BRIDGE NOTE preserved verbatim in input_required since it is a standing data-retention requirement independent of any single week's outcome, not a gate. ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION: step_id 7 collides with Onboarding step_id 7 (tal_channel, validation_type: enum, hold codes ['H-01','H-02'], flag codes ['F-05']). CHECKED, NOT ASSUMED IRRELEVANT: evaluateF05 has a real step_id===7 special case (value === 'msp' || value === 'partner', tied to Onboarding's channel-routing logic) — confirmed irrelevant to this step's content since this step's FLAG uses F-01, not F-05; F-05 would not even be invoked here. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived): evaluateH01 falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION, unrelated to a percentage-point shift. evaluateF01's step_id checks are ===2/4/18 only; step_id 7 matches none, falls through to bare return false — FAILS-OPEN, same shape as Steps 5/12's F-01 findings. category: A because Tier 2 names a required, non-optional escalation action.

steps.input_required: Analytics Lead queries the count of contacts at confidence_tier: MEDIUM or HIGH with differential_insufficient = False (§CA Section 2.2.2) per active solution category, via AEP segment query or operational dashboard, and compares to the prior week's count alongside the total active TAL account count for the same period. Time-boxed to 5 minutes per the corpus.
steps.validation_rule: Normal result: count is stable week-over-week, with any change proportional to the change in total active TAL account count for the same period (within 5%). Escalation condition: count drops more than 20% week-over-week while TAL account count is stable (within 5% change).
steps.why_this_matters: This check verifies the population feeding the two contact-level sales activation gates (confidence_tier ≥ MEDIUM, differential_insufficient = False, Document 8 §6.5) is healthy. This is the last check in the weekly sequence and the most consequential for revenue-facing operations — a silent drop here means BDRs and AEs stop receiving qualified contacts to act on, with no error visible anywhere except a quiet decline in sales activation volume.

hold_triggers.condition: Fires if the count of MEDIUM/HIGH confidence_tier, differential_insufficient: False contacts drops more than 20% week-over-week for any active solution category, while total active TAL account count is stable (within 5% change) over the same period. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer. A drop of this magnitude while TAL count is stable indicates signal collection degradation or a scoring pipeline anomaly, not a natural visitor behavior shift. Provide the week-over-week count, the TAL account count for both weeks, and the affected solution categories. The Platform Engineer investigates before the next weekly review.

hold_triggers_pending.condition: Fires if the count of MEDIUM/HIGH confidence_tier, differential_insufficient: False contacts drops more than 20% week-over-week for any active solution category, while total active TAL account count is stable (within 5% change) over the same period. Next action: escalate to Platform Engineer. A drop of this magnitude while TAL count is stable indicates signal collection degradation or a scoring pipeline anomaly, not a natural visitor behavior shift. Provide the week-over-week count, the TAL account count for both weeks, and the affected solution categories. The Platform Engineer investigates before the next weekly review.

steps.deferral_consequence: Cannot be deferred. This is the final check in the weekly sequence and the only one that directly verifies the population reaching sales activation — deferring it risks an undetected gate-population collapse persisting silently into the next week's sales operations.
steps._note: SINGLE-CONDITION TOPOLOGY (same shape as Steps 2 and 4): one comparison, one precisely-defined numeric threshold (>20% drop while TAL count stable, stability itself defined as within 5%) — no corpus gap, no tiering, no independent-condition split. This is the LAST of the eight Monitoring checks; Document 8 §5.1 frames the whole sequence as parallel, not sequential, but this check's data dependency (confidence_tier, differential_insufficient) makes it a natural closing check since it reflects the cumulative health of everything upstream (stitching, staleness, classification accuracy) that the prior seven checks examine individually. ONBOARDING-SIDE COLLISION CHARACTERIZATION: step_id 8 collides with Onboarding step_id 8 (tal_new_logo_eligible, validation_type: None, hold codes ['H-02'], flag codes ['F-02']). CHECKED: no step_id===8 special case exists anywhere in flag-hold-evaluator.ts for ANY evaluator function — confirmed by direct grep, not assumed; pure generic-fallthrough collision. MONITORING-SIDE GATE IMPACT (re-derived): this step's H-01 dispatches to evaluateH01 (Onboarding step 8 has no H-01 of its own; dispatch is by CODE, not by the colliding step's code inventory) — falls through to isNullish(value) — MIS-FIRES-WRONG-CONDITION, unrelated to a week-over-week percentage drop. category: A because the corpus names a required, non-optional escalation action with no documented-exception path. No FLAG described in the corpus for this check.
